title: Cellino Law | Google Reviews on Attorney Pages
description:
this request will require a scope to proceed. I don't believe the cost is worth the change, especially since Cellino will be undergoing a complete redesign this year.
 
Client Proposed changes
  • Add reviews section to attorney bio pages. This would need a design from Sky. The easiest implementation is probably a slides at the very bottom of the page. Responsive designs will need to be considered. I don't see any existing sliders on the site that could be used. This website will likely be a difficult one to work with.
  • Client would like the reviews to be able to automatically feed from Google, and filter by attorney name. This is possible, but won't be an easy task. I believe we do have functionality built to accomplish this, but not on this site. Since we did not build much of this site, it will likely take significant time to integrate.






Original Request (AM):

Hi Justyna! 

We are curious if you can review this and run this by the dev team to see what it would entail. If this requires a scope discussion, please let us know. 

Cellino Law wants to incorporate 2-3 relevant reviews (aka reviews pertaining to the attorney) from Google for each attorney on their site and their specific questions were:
  • Can they be fed in from Google directly to their bio pages? 
  • And how can we incorporate the reviews onto their bio pages without looking cumbersome? 
Currently, there are 28 attorneys listed on the site.


Original Request (Client):

Hey Fedeline,
 
I have another request/question for the web team.
 
Annmarie has asked for us to incorporate 2-3 relevant reviews from Google for each attorney on our site. My questions:
  • Is there a relatively easy way for us to feed in reviews from Google?
  • And/or, how can we incorporate these reviews on the bio pages without it looking cumbersome?

title: Next Steps for the AI script
description:
The latest update with the script was that Keith ran the script again, and we gave them the new content, telling them it was the first batch, and asked for feedback. 

Jon's feedback was: 
"I would still like to see more improvements on making these conversational and incorporate some personality.
The basic content is fine, but some of the content comes across at times as a little bland.
The copy tone/personality I would push these towards would be authentic, approachable, a confident, tireless ally.
Also, would it be possible to vary the format? All of these are structured basically the same. A bunch of text + bullet points.

description:

I’m currently in the process of updating our website tracking numbers and replacing all hardcoded tracking numbers from EverConvert with our “swap numbers”. I unfortunately do NOT have access to the JS code that EverConvert may have installed on the site, but this is the code that is CURRENT and should not be removed/should replace any other CallRail JS on the site:


<script type="text/javascript" src="cdn.callrail.com/companies/888358582/7d91ac1d56f26e9e4ed2/12/swap.js"></script>

Thank you,


Jill
